subject: [POLICY-9,POLICY-18] warnings + Controller hooks
commit_message:
[POLICY-9,POLICY-18] warnings + Controller hooks

1. clean up policy-utils warnings.  drools-pdp project has
   no warnings now.

2. add new Policy Controller hooks for feature programmability.

Change-Id: Ie991320e23e73118b235018d15ea66340a841a89
Signed-off-by: Jorge Hernandez <jh1730@att.com>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I do have a concern about giving features the ability to cause an early exit from "system" operations -- returning 'true' from some of these hook methods seem like they could leave the system in an inconsistent state. Should some of these be notify only, and have a 'void' return value?

Patch Set 3:

Patch Set 3:

Ralph, I had the same concern, I decided when I wrote it to provide higher programability tooling over tighter system aspects control as guiding principle at this stage (for now, this may change as we get more mature).   This is though, part of a more generic problem, in general, with any interception architecture.   For example, when performing a callback, the calling thread can be hijacked, or take arbitrarily long times, or exit the system, just to say ..   Then need to do the invokations from thread pools, measure feature invokation times, etc .. as countermeasure.   We can revisit in the future.   At least for the time being, we should take the assumption that loaded modules are trusted and safe for execution, they have been previously tested and validated.

Patch Set 3:

Following up on my previous comment, it would be nice the capability to remove a feature at runtime (on a temporary basis), so I can go through admin interface and just remove it, if it has funny behavior.

Patch Set 3:

Umm, don't see those warnings myself.  I use Eclipse IDE for Java Developers.   I suspect you may be using a JEE version that have JPA plugins and will be complaining about it.  I don't think those files should be there but will need to talk to others on why they are there.  In any case, let's not deal with those for this code inspection.
Change has been successfully merged by Jorge Hernandez
